name: Dagger CI/CD Engine API
what_it_does: Portable CI/CD engine with GraphQL API — lets developers write pipelines in their language of choice (Go, Python, TypeScript) that run identically locally and in any CI system, with content-addressed caching and container-native execution via BuildKit.
use_cases: Writing CI/CD pipelines in Go/Python/TypeScript that run locally and in GitHub Actions/CircleCI identically
Replacing complex bash CI scripts with typed, testable pipeline code via Dagger SDK
Building container images with automatic layer caching and BuildKit acceleration
Orchestrating multi-step deployment pipelines with parallel execution and dependency graphs
Sharing reusable pipeline modules via Dagger's module registry (Daggerverse)
not_for: Teams happy with YAML-based CI (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) without code reuse needs
Simple single-step pipelines that don't benefit from pipeline-as-code
Organizations needing managed CI with SLA guarantees (Dagger is an engine, not a platform)
best_when: A DevOps team wants CI/CD pipelines that run locally, are testable, share logic across projects, and run consistently across any CI platform.
avoid_when: You want simple YAML CI configuration, don't need local pipeline development, or prefer a fully managed CI platform with its own UI.

auth.notes: Dagger Cloud token for telemetry and traces. Engine itself has no auth — local daemon. Dagger Cloud for team features and trace storage. Engine-to-registry auth uses Docker credential helpers. No auth needed for local execution.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free; Dagger Cloud: free for individual developers
pricing.paid_tiers: Dagger Cloud Teams from $30/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source engine — free forever for self-use. Dagger Cloud provides trace visualization and team features. Founded by Solomon Hykes (original Docker creator). Strong momentum and backing. Daggerverse for sharing pipeline modules.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local engine; Dagger Cloud has API limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API is primary interface (via SDK wrappers). Multi-language SDKs abstract GraphQL. Agent can call Dagger pipelines programmatically via SDK. Dagger module system (Daggerverse) enables reusable pipeline components. Excellent for agent-driven CI orchestration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content-addressed caching — identical inputs always produce cached outputs. Pipeline operations fully idempotent with caching. Fresh runs bypass cache with --no-cache flag.
known_agent_gotchas: Requires Docker/container runtime — not suitable for environments without Docker
GraphQL API requires SDK to use effectively — raw GraphQL is verbose
BuildKit daemon must be running — startup time adds latency to first operation
Module versioning in Daggerverse — pin module versions for reproducibility

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable. Container isolation for all pipeline steps. Secret API for passing credentials without leaking to logs. BuildKit's content-addressable storage. No network access by default in containers (configurable). Solomon Hykes (Docker creator) — strong security culture.
